human_verification.notes: Reviewed vehicle-logistics outputs for VIN identity discipline, yard and transit evidence handling, carrier and supplier escalation posture, quality and release boundary discipline, and refusal of dispatch and approval overreach.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicitly limited to automotive vehicle-logistics coordination, handoff ownership, blocker tracking, carrier and vendor-risk escalation, and closure management.

Authoritative sources cover automotive quality governance, VIN identity, finished-vehicle logistics terminology, transport-status messaging, traceability, and recall or release boundaries with refresh discipline.

Research scenario families confirm traceability conflict handling, quality hold and release-boundary handling, supplier or substitution escalation, missing-context handoff refusal, duplicated ownership, and execution-boundary refusal.

Evaluation scenario set covers missing artifacts, duplicated ownership, traceability conflict, quality hold escalation, unapproved substitution, execution refusal, low-confidence escalation, policy conflict, and closure verification.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based with explicit promotion criteria for a retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities were left in the research contract coverage.
Boundary handling remained strong across VIN traceability, release holds, supplier and carrier substitution, duplicated ownership, and execution-refusal cases.

notes: This pack is designed for validated deployment as a spec-pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
